posts.name: Wesley Thomas Terrill
posts.text:
Dear combine experts,
I am trying to create unblinded impacts plots for the background-only hypothesis.
Currently I tried running:
combineTool.py -M Impacts -d card_TChiWZ_Disp-neg_200_195_0_allEEMM.root -m 200 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 -n impacts_expSig0 --expectSignal=0

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d card_TChiWZ_Disp-neg_200_195_0_allEEMM.root -m 200 --robustFit 1 --doFits -n impacts_expSig0 --expectSignal=0 --parallel 8

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d card_TChiWZ_Disp-neg_200_195_0_allEEMM.root -m 200 --robustFit 1 --output impacts_expSig0.json -n impacts_expSig0 --parallel 4 --expectSignal=0

plotImpacts.py -i impacts_expSig0.json -o impacts_expSig0

but this seems to create identical impacts plots as to either not specifying --expectSignal or using --expectSignal=1.
Any help would be greatly appreciated!
posts.cooked_html:
Dear combine experts,

I am trying to create unblinded impacts plots for the background-only hypothesis.

Currently I tried running:

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d card_TChiWZ_Disp-neg_200_195_0_allEEMM.root -m 200 --doInitialFit --robustFit 1 -n impacts_expSig0 --expectSignal=0

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d card_TChiWZ_Disp-neg_200_195_0_allEEMM.root -m 200 --robustFit 1 --doFits -n impacts_expSig0 --expectSignal=0 --parallel 8

combineTool.py -M Impacts -d card_TChiWZ_Disp-neg_200_195_0_allEEMM.root -m 200 --robustFit 1 --output impacts_expSig0.json -n impacts_expSig0 --parallel 4 --expectSignal=0

plotImpacts.py -i impacts_expSig0.json -o impacts_expSig0

but this seems to create identical impacts plots as to either not specifying --expectSignal or using --expectSignal=1.

Any help would be greatly appreciated!

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi,
--freezeParameters r --setParameters r=0 should work. (I assume that background is for r=0)
ExpectedSignal will influence only the asimov dataset or the toy datasets.
-Andrea
posts.cooked_html:
Hi,

--freezeParameters r --setParameters r=0 should work. (I assume that background is for r=0)

ExpectedSignal will influence only the asimov dataset or the toy datasets.

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Hi Wesley,
Perhaps just to add: for the impacts to run there needs to be a parameter of interest (see this documentation page for more details on what the impacts mean).
So while you can calculate impacts on a different parameter than r (if there is another freely floating parameter in your model), but you may need to think a bit about exactly what ‘unblinded background-only hypothesis impacts’ mean for your model, and whether it is really going to give you the information that you want.
Best,
Adinda
